interpretation: The Union of European Football Associations technical observer panel singled out his control of the game.
Season-long Champions League passing volume shows why Paris could sustain possession through midfield.
He was not just recycling possession; he repeatedly moved the ball through pressure lines.
High build-up involvement fits the final's control profile.
Ball-carrying gave Paris another way to advance play when passing lanes narrowed.
He remained involved late, then Paris used Lucas Beraldo as a shootout option after substituting him.
